SEARCH
Module it.polimi.ingsw.am37
Package it.polimi.ingsw.am37.model.player

Class Kingdom

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am37.model.player.Kingdom
public class Kingdom extends Object
The Kingdom class contains all the information and behaviour of the play area of a Player. The play area is where the Player places his cards.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Kingdom(StartCard startCard, Side startCardSide)
    The Kingdom(startCard, startCardSide) constructor

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<Position>
    getActivePositions()
    The getActivePositions() method returns the list containing all the Position currently active in the Kingdom, meaning that a Card can be placed here.
    List<Position>
    getImpossiblePositions()
    The getImpossiblePositions() method returns the list containing all the Position where it is impossible to place a Card, meaning that during the entire game, the Player will never be able to place a Card here.
    Hashtable<Resource,Integer>
    getOnFieldResources()
    The getOnFieldResources() method returns the table containing the current Resources of the Player.
    List<Side>
    getPlacedSides()
    The getPlacedSides() method returns the list containing all the Sides placed by the Player.
    String[][]
    getVisual(boolean utfActive)
    the getVisual method prints the kingdom of the player based on the encoding chosen.
    void
    updateKingdom(StandardCard card, Side sidePlaced, Position position)
    The updateKingdom(sidePlaced, position) method is called every time the player successfully places a Card and invokes private methods that update the current state of the Kingdom.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Kingdom

      public Kingdom(StartCard startCard, Side startCardSide)
      The Kingdom(startCard, startCardSide) constructor
      Parameters:
      startCard - The StartCard that the Player wants to place in the Kingdom.
      startCardSide - The Side of the StartCard that the Player wants to place.

  • Method Details

    • getOnFieldResources

      public Hashtable<Resource,Integer> getOnFieldResources()
      The getOnFieldResources() method returns the table containing the current Resources of the Player.
      Returns:
      The onFieldResources attribute.

    • getPlacedSides

      public List<Side> getPlacedSides()
      The getPlacedSides() method returns the list containing all the Sides placed by the Player.
      Returns:
      The placedSides attribute.

    • getActivePositions

      public List<Position> getActivePositions()
      The getActivePositions() method returns the list containing all the Position currently active in the Kingdom, meaning that a Card can be placed here.
      Returns:
      The activePositions attribute.

    • getImpossiblePositions

      public List<Position> getImpossiblePositions()
      The getImpossiblePositions() method returns the list containing all the Position where it is impossible to place a Card, meaning that during the entire game, the Player will never be able to place a Card here.
      Returns:
      The impossiblePositions method.

    • updateKingdom

      public void updateKingdom(StandardCard card, Side sidePlaced, Position position)
      The updateKingdom(sidePlaced, position) method is called every time the player successfully places a Card and invokes private methods that update the current state of the Kingdom.
      Parameters:
      card - The Card that the Player has placed down.
      sidePlaced - The Side of the Card that the player has placed.
      position - The Position where the Card is placed.

    • getVisual

      public String[][] getVisual(boolean utfActive)
      the getVisual method prints the kingdom of the player based on the encoding chosen.
      Parameters:
      utfActive - tells if the encoding is enabled or not.
      Returns:
      a string representing the kingdom of the player.
